frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Bro honestly I never get this makeup scene. If someone is naturally pretty then why put on so much that she looks different, and if she comes totally plain then people say she doesn’t even maintain herself for office. Simple rehna is best, but some girls overdo it only for attention.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticized for wearing 'too much' makeup and also for not putting in enough effort. It encodes appearance policing, 'natural beauty' preference, and the assumption that makeup is done for male attention.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
